pokemon:lopunny.name: Lopunny
pokemon:lopunny.summary: The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.
The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.
Extremely cautious, it quickly bounds off when it senses danger.
An extremely cautious Pokémon. It cloaks its body with its fluffy ear fur when it senses danger.
It sheds its fur twice a year. Its winter fur is soft and fluffy.
Its fur is warm and yet remarkably light. This Pokémon kicks as though it were a master of karate, driving back its opponents with ease.
Extremely cautious, it quickly bounds off when it senses danger.
It is very conscious of its looks and never fails to groom its ears. It runs with sprightly jumps.
The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.
Once hot seasons are over, Lopunny’s coat will be replaced with fur that holds a lot of insulating air in preparation for colder weather.
It sheds its fur twice a year. Its winter fur is soft and fluffy.
Lopunny is constantly monitoring its surroundings. If danger approaches, this Pokémon responds with superdestructive kicks.
It’s notably wary and has a dislike of fighting, but at the same time, it can deliver powerful kicks with its lithe legs.
Lopunny regrows its coat twice a year. Mufflers and hats made from its fur are really warm.
The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.
Extremely cautious, it quickly bounds off when it senses danger.
Extremely cautious, it quickly bounds off when it senses danger.
The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.

pokemon:buneary.name: Buneary
pokemon:buneary.summary: When it senses danger, it perks up its ears. On cold nights, it sleeps with its head tucked into its fur.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: When it senses danger, it perks up its ears. On cold nights, it sleeps with its head tucked into its fur.
Its ears are always rolled up. They can be forcefully extended to shatter even a large boulder.
By extending its rolled-up ears and striking the ground, it can bound so high it surprises itself.
It slams foes by sharply uncoiling its rolled ears. It stings enough to make a grown-up cry in pain.
You can tell how it feels by the way it rolls its ears. When it’s scared, both ears are rolled up.
My hypothesis as to why Buneary rolls up its ears is that its hearing is far too keen. I surmise that the Pokémon protects its hearing by limiting the sound that may enter its ears.
Its ears are always rolled up. They can be forcefully extended to shatter even a large boulder.
When it senses danger, it perks up its ears. On cold nights, it sleeps with its head tucked into its fur.
Its ears are always rolled up. They can be forcefully extended to shatter even a large boulder.
Buneary can attack by rolling up their ears and then striking with the force created by unrolling them. This attack becomes stronger with training.
You can tell how it feels by the way it rolls its ears. When it’s scared, both ears are rolled up.
If both of Buneary’s ears are rolled up, something is wrong with its body or mind. It’s a sure sign the Pokémon is in need of care.
The reason it keeps one ear rolled up is so it can launch a swift counterattack if it’s attacked by an enemy.
Its arms and legs are weak, but when it rolls its ears up tight and then unleashes them with its full force, it can smash boulders to dust.
Its ears are always rolled up. They can be forcefully extended to shatter even a large boulder.
By extending its rolled-up ears and striking the ground, it can bound so high it surprises itself.
Its ears are always rolled up. They can be forcefully extended to shatter even a large boulder.
When it senses danger, it perks up its ears. On cold nights, it sleeps with its head tucked into its fur.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: When it senses danger, it perks up its ears. On cold nights, it sleeps with its head tucked into its fur.
